strategic_profile.executive_summary: The University of Louisville is a preeminent public metropolitan research university located in Kentucky. Known for its accessible admissions, massive first-generation student population, and deep integration with regional corporate giants like UPS, UofL serves as a primary driver of economic mobility and R1 medical innovation in the state.

analyst_takeaways.insight: High emphasis on pre-professional and STEM pathways.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Engineering and Nursing are highly impacted and require strict prerequisites, whereas general admission to Arts & Sciences is highly accessible.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Unique corporate partnerships offer massive tuition subsidies.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Programs like UPS Metropolitan College allow students to graduate debt-free by working night shifts, though this contributes to lower 4-year graduation rates.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Urban campus deeply intertwined with ACC Athletics.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students experience a traditional 'big state school' sports culture while benefiting from urban internship access.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Vibrant and diverse student body heavily engaged in Greek life, over 300 student organizations, and ACC sports tailgating. Strong sense of community despite being a large urban commuter-friendly campus.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Urban setting allows for extensive local transit options and immediate access to downtown Louisville internships.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Classified as an R1 institution, UofL boasts world-class medical research facilities, having pioneered innovations like the first self-contained artificial heart transplant.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional for all majors. Applicants without test scores undergo a comprehensive transcript review. Direct admission to Nursing or Engineering without scores requires high grades in advanced math and science.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Undergraduate waitlist data not publicly disclosed. Yield rate calculated based on historical Peterson's dataset estimates.

gpa.notes: Average incoming high school GPA is approximately 3.60.

testing.policy_note: ACT submission rate is approximately 85-88%; SAT submission rate is 13-18%.

colleges.name: J.B. Speed School of Engineering
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Applicants must demonstrate strong proficiency in quantitative sciences. Those without test scores must have B- or higher in Pre-Calculus and Chemistry.
colleges.housing_profile: Traditional on-campus residence halls and specific Living-Learning Communities for engineers.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Internal transfers require B- or higher in Pre-Calculus.
majors.notes: Program allows computer science study without the strict physical science requirements of a B.S., requiring 30 hours in another area of study.

colleges.name: School of Nursing
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students dedicated to healthcare who can maintain high academic standards in initial prerequisite sciences.
colleges.housing_profile: Access to Health Sciences Center resources.

majors.name: Nursing
majors.special_requirements: Two-step admission process. Must apply to upper division after 3-4 semesters with no more than two NUR course failures.
majors.notes: Requires rigorous lower-division math and science performance.

application_deadlines.notes: Admission operates on a rolling basis; priority deadlines exist in the fall for competitive merit scholarships.

supplemental_requirements.details: Requires a 400-word essay on career goals, two letters of recommendation, and a resume.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: General admission is metrics-driven, but holistic review (essays/resume) is invoked for borderline applicants or those applying test-optional to competitive tracks.

major_selection_tactics: Apply to non-impacted Arts & Sciences programs if test scores do not meet Engineering minimums.
Utilize the Exploratory track to fulfill foundational math/chemistry requirements before initiating an internal transfer.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Applying to a BA in Computer Science or a generic exploratory BA allows entry to the university; after earning a 2.8 GPA and a B- in MATH 111, students can successfully transfer into the Speed School.

scholarships.name: Out-of-State Base Grant
scholarships.benefits: Effectively brings out-of-state tuition to the in-state equivalent level.

scholarships.name: Grawemeyer Competitive Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Funds undergraduate research and civic engagement.

scholarships.name: Breonna Taylor Legacy Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Provides undergraduate funding with preference for African-American Kentucky residents.

scholarships.name: UPS Earn & Learn (Metropolitan College)
scholarships.benefits: 100% in-state tuition coverage for students working the third shift at UPS Worldport.

ap_policy.general_rule: Awards credit for scores of 3, 4, or 5.
exceptions: School of Medicine pre-med prerequisites will not accept AP Biology credit.
ap_policy.usage: Used for course equivalents and elective credits.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Engineering requires minimum 'C-' grades on all transferred courses.

what_it_takes: Ability to balance work and school, especially for those in UPS Metroversity.
Strong academic foundations in math for STEM prospects.
essay_tips: Demonstrate a clear connection to the state of Kentucky for specialized scholarships.
red_flags: Poor performance in high school math (instant rejection for direct engineering admits).
insights: Campus feels integrated with the city but requires situational awareness regarding urban safety.
Flipped classrooms are highly common in the science and engineering departments.
